content: Lyme disease: clinical diagnosis if classic EM rash in endemic area; otherwise two-tier serology (EIA/ELISA followed by Western blot if positive/equivocal); PCR from synovial fluid/CSF in select cases. Malaria: thick and thin blood smears (gold standard, allows species ID and parasitemia quantification); rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs) detect antigens (HRP2 for P. falciparum, pLDH/aldolase for pan-malaria); repeat smears q12-24h if initial negative but high suspicion. Dengue: NS1 antigen (acute phase days 1-7), IgM/IgG serology (IgM positive after day 5), RT-PCR for viral RNA. Travel history critical for diagnosis. Prophylaxis recommendations: malaria chemoprophylaxis based on destination resistance patterns (atovaquone-proguanil, doxycycline, mefloquine, tafenoquine); tick bite prevention for Lyme (permethrin-treated clothing, DEET, tick checks).
